seniority.level: Senior IC (likely Staff TPM or equivalent)
level_signals: Compensation range $365K–$435K base, well above standard mid-level
'Several years of experience in technical program management'
Expected to develop and improve launch processes and playbooks that scale
Works closely with research PMs and engineering leads; presents to leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have several years of experience in technical program management, with a track record of successfully delivering complex, cross-functional programs
Possess deep technical knowledge that allows you to engage meaningfully with ML researchers and engineers
Have experience managing programs with significant external dependencies and multiple stakeholder groups
Excel at translating between technical teams and stakeholders, making complex tradeoffs understandable
Can thrive in ambiguous situations, bringing structure to complex technical challenges
Have strong organizational skills and can manage multiple parallel workstreams effectively
Are comfortable operating at a fast pace where priorities shift and new challenges emerge quickly
Have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence without authority
Build trust quickly and maintain strong relationships even under pressure
Are passionate about Anthropic's mission and interested in the challenges of bringing frontier AI capabilities to users safely
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

scope.surface: Model and product launches at Anthropic — coordinating research, engineering, product, infrastructure, and go-to-market teams
scope.business_model: AI research and products company, B2B and B2C
primary_metrics_inferred: Cross-functional alignment and unblocking of teams
Quality and pace of model/product launches
core_responsibilities: Lead end-to-end program management for model and product launches
Drive cross-functional alignment and decision-making across workstreams
Partner with Research PMs and engineering leads to sequence work, manage dependencies, and navigate technical tradeoffs
Track and communicate program status, risks, and dependencies to leadership and stakeholders
Build strong relationships with technical stakeholders
Coordinate with research teams to understand upcoming model capabilities and prepare launch plans
Navigate tradeoffs between speed, quality, and scope
Develop and improve launch processes, playbooks, and operational frameworks
Create and maintain documentation of launch processes, decision logs, and cross-functional dependencies

compensation.equity_form: optional equity donation matching mentioned (suggests options/RSUs are part of package)

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($365K–$435K base)
Clear scope: end-to-end launch program management for a high-impact AI company
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every single qualification
Explicit commitment to diversity and inclusion in hiring
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% in-office time in San Francisco — may not suit fully remote candidates
Years of experience required not precisely stated ('several years') — seniority bar is ambiguous
Very high compensation range ($365K–$435K) may signal expectations for a very senior bar that is not fully enumerated in the JD
No specific technical domain named (ML infra, systems, etc.) — 'deep technical knowledge' is vague and could be interpreted broadly or narrowly

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + scope of responsibilities)
seniority.years_required_min (not stated; 'several years' is not quantified)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (inferred as San Francisco from HQ mention; JD does not explicitly name city for this role)
ambiguities: 'Several years of experience' is not quantified — could mean 3, 5, or 7+ years depending on internal leveling
JD does not specify which technical domains count as 'deep technical knowledge' — ML infrastructure, systems engineering, and AI research all plausible

All requirements are framed as 'You may be a good fit if you...' rather than hard requirements, creating ambiguity about which are truly mandatory vs. preferred — treated as hard given unconditional phrasing in context
